carbon_policy_signal.item: EU weakens methane enforcement rules under US pressure and floats energy-security exemptions from methane compliance; reduces supply-side cost burden on gas importers and producers.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Looser methane enforcement lowers marginal gas import cost; cheaper gas input weakens power-generation fuel-switch incentive to coal/nuclear, compressing merit-order margins and EUA burn.

risk_flags: EU storage at 34.63% (11th pctile, 12.5pp below 5yr seasonal) — refill pace into summer will define H2 power risk and TTF floor.
GB Power 93rd pctile (126.73 EUR/MWh) with -7.99% daily swing — Brexit/interconnect/wind volatility persists; watch 10–20% daily moves.
Iran war disrupts Hormuz; US naval operation ongoing (days horizon). Success → crude relief, TTF downside ±5–8%; escalation → LNG premium +8–12%.
Clean Spark 39.27 EUR/MWh (92nd pctile, +23.73 YTD) — gas firmly in-the-money vs. coal; coal at 36th pctile. Merit order heavily favours CCGTs; coal dispatch risk if gas reprices up.

base.summary: Storage squeeze persists; US clears Hormuz partially; gas stable, power premium holds.

downside.summary: US naval operation succeeds, Hormuz reopens; crude rallies reverse; methane rules soften supply costs.

upside.summary: Iran war escalates, Hormuz closes; LNG premium re-asserts; storage refill stalls further.

themes: GB-DE power premium, tight storage below seasonal
Iran war LNG/Hormuz upside vs. Hormuz clearance downside
EU methane rule softening signals cheaper marginal gas
Clean Spark at 92nd pctile—gas in-the-money vs. coal
top_drivers: GB Power 93rd pctile + DE Power 76th pctile: tight storage (11th pctile, 12.5pp below seasonal) and wind/interconnect volatility locking in thermal premium.
Clean Spark 92nd pctile (39.27 EUR/MWh): gas firmly in-the-money vs. coal merit order; coal at 36th pctile. CCGTs dispatch-dominant.
Iran war + Hormuz blockade: LNG premium risk upside (+8–12% TTF on escalation), but US naval intervention signals crude relief and downside power risk (−5–8%).
top_takeaway: GB and DE power at multi-year highs (93rd/76th pctile) amid storage 12.5pp below seasonal; Iran war geopolitics underpin LNG/Hormuz premium, but US naval intervention signals crude relief and downside power risk.

watchlist.metric: Hormuz strait clearance / US naval operation outcome
watchlist.why: Days-horizon geopolitical binary. Success removes Brent/LNG premium; failure entrenches risk premium into TTF and DE/GB power. Crude >100 USD/bbl locks fuel-switch regime.
Currently 12.5pp below seasonal, at 11th pctile. Weekly +0.35% insufficient; need +2–3% weekly to rebuild. Lag persists → summer power tightness and TTF support.

watchlist.metric: EU methane rules finalization & exemption scope
watchlist.why: Weeks horizon. Looser enforcement reduces supply-cost burden on LNG imports; indirect TTF downside ±3–5% if exemptions are broad. Regulatory signal for future carbon stringency.
Weeks–months. Limited near-term relief; pipeline capacity constraints. Low probability of material supply addition within 6 weeks; more of a 2027+ story.

watchlist_dated.name: US Hormuz naval operation status update
watchlist_dated.why: Geopolitical binary on crude/LNG premium; direct transmission to TTF and DE/GB power via fuel-switch arb.
